title: nepeta cephalotes
intro: Nepeta cephalotes, also known as catmint, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) with a tall, upright growth habit and blue to purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Nepeta cephalotes include its ability to ease anxiety and insomnia, improve cognitive function, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as asthma and bronchitis. Nepeta cephalotes can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to support overall well-being and promote a restful night's sleep.
